name: mailer
description: Reusable email sending (nodemailer). Resolves SMTP from the admin-configured DB settings when auth is installed, else SMTP_* env, else logs to the console. Used by auth and contact-form; the transport is overridable from app.extensions.ts.
env: # With auth installed, configure SMTP on the admin Settings page (stored
# encrypted in the DB, applied live). These env vars are a fallback/override;
# with no SMTP configured at all the mailer logs messages to the console.
# Local dev uses Mailpit: docker compose --profile dev up → http://localhost:8025

inject.text:
### mailer
- Send email with `import { sendMail, actionEmail } from "../mail/mailer"` (path relative to your file). `sendMail({ to, subject, text, html? })`.
- SMTP comes from the admin Settings page (when auth is installed) or `SMTP_*` env; with neither, messages are logged to the console (dev links are grabbable from logs).
- To use a provider SDK instead of SMTP, call `setMailTransport(...)` from `apps/api/src/app.extensions.ts`.

instructions: Send mail from anywhere in the API:  import { sendMail } from "../mail/mailer"  then  await sendMail({ to, subject, text }).

SMTP is optional: with auth installed, configure it on the admin Settings page (DB, applied live); otherwise set SMTP_HOST/SMTP_PORT/MAIL_FROM in .env. With none set, messages are logged to the console.
To send through a provider SDK, override the transport in apps/api/src/app.extensions.ts:  import { setMailTransport } from "./mail/mailer";  setMailTransport(myTransport).
